source_jobs.title: Staff Software Engineer (Fullstack)
source_jobs.description:
About Us

Propel is on a mission to build technology that strengthens the social safety net. We’re a passionate team of ~100 Propellers who envision a future where every American has the tools and resources they need to thrive.

The Role

Are you a Fullstack Engineer looking to make a big impact at a mission-driven company? Do you thrive in high-autonomy startup environments? 

We’re looking for a motivated Fullstack Engineer to join our engineering team at Propel to help make this a reality. This role includes opportunities to flex between IC and engineering management and team leadership!

This role will make an impact and possibly work across several products and stacks, including interacting with government programs, surfacing savings opportunities, enrolling users in healthcare plans, internal tooling, architecture, and more! We are looking for a passionate, user-centric fullstack engineer that is excited about working on meaningful and complex technical problems that have a positive impact on our users’ lives.

Here's how you'll impact the company:

 - Be a strategic leader in identifying the best technical and product opportunities. Whether it’s hopping in to build a service from scratch to support a new product line or advocating for a large refactor of a legacy system, this role will have a strong voice in setting the technical and product direction of the company and be hands-on in making it happen. You will likely be asked to lead a team at various points and be flexible in going between impactful IC work and leadership responsibilities.

 - Up-level and set the bar for engineering. You will be a role model for others and establish best practices when it comes to technical decisions, code hygiene, written communication and documentation, and judgment when it comes to shipping high-quality products and projects on a timeline.

 - Dive deep into the most important, highest complexity problems at the company. When there is something that needs to get done at high complexity and scale, you’ll be someone we lean on to help get it done. You’ll be given both a lot of autonomy and a lot of support in how you want to tackle tough but rewarding challenges!

Requirements:

 - 10+ years experience in designing, building, shipping, and maintaining complex full-stack software at scale

 - 3+ years experience successfully leading and managing a team

 - Fluency in at least one backend and one frontend language, with a willingness to learn Python and JavaScript

 - Experience working in a startup environment and possess the ability to operate effectively and independently when faced with ambiguity

 - Ability to ramp up quickly to our product ecosystem to contribute to large and impactful projects

 - Excellent communication skills across technical and non-technical audiences and topics, both written and verbal

 - Ability to provide excellent feedback in technical specs, code reviews, and team discussions

 - Passion for making a difference in the lives of our users

Exceptional candidates will have:

 - A track record of identifying the right problems to solve, advocating for and driving change, and keeping stakeholders in the loop

 - Experience with building consumer-facing product(s) for large user bases

 - Flexibility in operating as a pure individual contributor or as a manager of a small team

 - Curiosity and excitement when confronted with new technologies, stacks, or novel problems

 - Excellent judgment when trading off speed of shipping vs. robustness and scalability

 - The ability to adapt and communicate smoothly across all levels of seniority

 - A focus on impact and ability to prioritize both long-term and short term needs

 - Eagerness to participate at the strategic level when it comes to the technical and product roadmap and prioritization on the organization-level

 - Passion and skill for mentoring and up-leveling other engineers

At Propel, you'll enjoy:

 - Meaningful work and a strong, shared sense of mi

source_jobs.title: Senior Full Stack Engineer, Healthcare
source_jobs.description:
About Us

Propel builds technology that strengthens the social safety net in America. We envision a future where every American has the tools and resources they need to thrive.

The safety net’s strength depends not just on funding, but delivery. Propel exists to dramatically improve how government benefits are delivered. Managing SNAP benefits, picking a D-SNP plan, or protecting against EBT theft should feel as seamless as Apple Pay or Netflix.

Governments struggle to build modern, scalable user experiences. We don’t.

Over 5.5 million Americans trust Propel today. We’ve grown revenue 35% YoY and are reinventing entire benefit categories, including Medicare, around the real needs of low-income Americans. We’re building a high-growth, high-ceiling, sustainable for-profit company where impact drives revenue.

The Role

Are you a Senior Fullstack Engineer looking to build 0→1 products at a mission-driven company? Do you enjoy creating consumer-grade experiences and thrive in high-autonomy startup environments?

We’re looking for a motivated Senior Fullstack Engineer to join Propel and help build new Medicare and Medicaid products from the ground up. In this role, you’ll work end-to-end across frontend and backend systems to develop user experiences that help people understand coverage options, surface savings opportunities, and enroll in healthcare plans. 

You’ll collaborate closely with product and design, and help shape technical architecture, internal tooling, and scalable integrations with healthcare and government systems.




What we offer:

 - An engineering culture that is humble, motivated, and collaborative 

 - Consistent knowledge sharing through projects or bugs demos, Lunch & Learn presentations, and documentation

 - A people-first approach to management where we value engineers’ voices, support career development, and offer opportunities for technical growth that interest you

 - Meaningful work and a strong shared sense of mission

 - A remote-first working environment. Our headquarters is in Brooklyn and we have a co-working monthly stipend for employees outside of NYC. We're currently accepting applications from the states we have an established entity, which includes California, Colorado, District of Columbia, Florida, Georgia, Illinois, Maryland, Massachusetts, New Jersey, New York, Oregon, Pennsylvania, Texas, Virginia, and Washington.

 - 20 Days of PTO

 - 4% 401K match

 - $10k lifetime spend towards Carrot Fertility

 - Excellent Medical, Dental and Vision options

 - Fair, equitable, and competitive salaries. Propel believes that everyone should be compensated fairly and equitably. We set our salary ranges using compensation data from hundreds of NYC based startups at our stage. Additionally, pay is not determined based on location. The salary range for this position is $170,000 - $240,000 depending on experience and interview performance. Equity and excellent benefits come with this position as well!

What you will do:

 - Build software systems used by millions of low-income Americans, helping to put dollars back in their pockets

 - Build iOS and Android apps using Vue + TypeScript + Capacitor

 - Develop significant server-side software using Python and Flask, backed by Postgres and Redis; leverage AWS offerings as well as other open source technologies

 - Create software that is reliable, scalable, and maintainable

 - Effectively communicate with team members during projects, code review, etc.

 - Contribute to building our engineering culture and processes as we continue to scale

 - Collaborate on deciding on the right projects, building great solutions, and helping your team succeed

 - Work with our Product and Design team to scope projects and execute on innovative products

 - Participate in our on-call rotation

What you have:

 - 5+ years of experience designing, building, shipping, and maintaining complex full-stack software

 - Experience with a startup environment or the ability to

source_jobs.title: Senior Platform Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Us

Propel is on a mission to build technology that strengthens the social safety net. We’re a passionate team of ~100 Propellers who envision a future where every American has the tools and resources they need to thrive.

The Role

Are you a Platform Engineer looking to make a big impact on a mission-driven company? Do you enjoy architecting software that shapes an entire engineering organization? Do you thrive in high-autonomy startup environments?

We’re looking for a self-motivated Senior Platform Engineer to join our Developer Enablement Team within the engineering org at Propel.

This role empowers other engineers by improving the cloud native platforms that enable development and delivery of Propel’s technology. These cloud native platforms impact several products and stacks, including interacting with government programs, surfacing savings opportunities, enrolling users in healthcare plans, internal tooling, architecture, and more! Your focus will be on running fault tolerant applications in Kubernetes and AWS, and solving organization-wide engineering problems. 

We are looking for a tinkerer to experiment with improvements to the developer experience at Propel. You will architect new features for our cloud platform, support a wide-variety of production applications, address technical debt, and help guide other engineers.

What we offer:

 - An engineering culture that is humble, motivated, and collaborative 

 - Consistent knowledge sharing through projects or bugs demos, Lunch & Learn presentations, and documentation

 - A people-first approach to management where we value engineers’ voices, support career development, and offer opportunities for technical growth that interest you

 - Meaningful work and a strong shared sense of mission

 - A remote-first working environment. Our headquarters is in Brooklyn and we have a co-working monthly stipend for employees outside of NYC. We're currently accepting applications from the states we have an established entity, which includes California, Colorado, District of Columbia, Florida, Georgia, Illinois, Maryland, Massachusetts, New Jersey, New York, Oregon, Pennsylvania, Texas, Virginia, and Washington.

 - Fair, equitable, and competitive salaries. Propel believes that everyone should be compensated fairly and equitably. We set our salary ranges using compensation data from hundreds of NYC based startups at our stage. Additionally, pay is not determined based on location. The salary range for this position is $170,000 - $240,000 depending on experience and interview performance. Equity and excellent benefits come with this position as well!

 - 20 Days of PTO

 - 4% 401K match

 - $10k lifetime spend towards Carrot Fertility

 - Excellent Medical, Dental and Vision options

What you will do:

 - Own our fundamental cloud services and tooling. You will manage our cloud networks, security policies, and other low-level cloud services. Our engineering team provisions cloud infrastructure on top of this foundation, using infrastructure as code tools like Terraform.

 - Own our application platform. You will administer our Kubernetes clusters to meet the needs of the engineering team. You will explore new tools and services that improve the security and reliability of our applications running on the platform.

 - Own our developer experience. You will assist engineers with best practices for using and deploying to the platform. You will administer our CI/CD and other internal tools to make this as easy as possible.

What you have:

 - 5+ years experience in DevOps, Site Reliability Engineering, Platform Engineering, or Systems Engineering

 - Proficient with at least one high-level programming language - Python, Go, or whatever else you like

 - Strong understanding of networking, containers, security and scalability

 - Strong Kubernetes fundamentals and experience with Helm or other common tools in the ecosystem

 - Strong infrastructure as code skills (we use Terraform

source_jobs.title: Medicare Advisor
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Propel builds technology that strengthens the social safety net in America. We envision a future where every American has the tools and resources they need to thrive.

The safety net’s strength depends not just on funding, but delivery. Propel exists to dramatically improve how government benefits are delivered. Managing SNAP benefits, picking a D-SNP plan, or protecting against EBT theft should feel as seamless as Apple Pay or Netflix.

Governments struggle to build modern, scalable user experiences. We don’t.

Over 5.5 million Americans trust Propel today. We’ve grown revenue 35% YoY and are reinventing entire benefit categories, including Medicare, around the real needs of low-income Americans. We’re building a high-growth, high-ceiling, sustainable for-profit company where impact drives revenue.


ABOUT THE ROLE

Propel serves approximately 1M users who are Medicare eligible, most of whom are also Medicaid eligible. We are building the first-ever app-based brokerage focused on helping "duals" (Americans on Medicare and Medicaid) enroll in healthcare plans. We specialize in Dual Eligible Special Needs Plans (D-SNPs) and are expanding to 30 states in 2026, with plans to move into larger Medicare markets over the next 24 months. As a Medicare Advisor, you will be a trusted expert, helping users compare Medicare plans, enroll with confidence, and maximize their healthcare benefits. You will also collaborate with our EPD (engineering, product, design) team to refine how we support users in navigating government healthcare.

This role requires someone who is comfortable using AI tools in their daily work to research plans, draft communications, summarize complex information, and improve personal productivity. You should be an enthusiastic and capable user of modern AI-powered tools.


WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Respond to incoming inquiries from users regarding D-SNPs, Traditional Medicare, Medicare Advantage plans, and Medicaid, providing expert guidance and support via in-app chat (primary) and phone (secondary).

 - Use AI-powered tools to efficiently research plan details, compare coverage options, and synthesize complex regulatory information for users.

 - Apply a user-centric approach, prioritizing user needs and exceeding service expectations.

 - Conduct comprehensive comparisons of Medicare Advantage plans, ensuring users understand their options for healthcare coverage based on their specific needs.

 - Educate users on eligibility requirements, enrollment processes, and Medicare compliance, helping them navigate the complexities of healthcare regulations.

 - Execute enrollment applications accurately, providing users with critical information about deadlines and ongoing actions needed to sustain their coverage effectively.

 - Maintain appointments with multiple insurance carriers across 30+ states and ensure compliance with licensing requirements as Propel expands its D-SNP footprint.

 - Collaborate with product, design, and engineering teams to improve app functionality, offering insights from user interactions and enrollment experiences.

 - Leverage AI tools to draft user communications, create summaries, and identify patterns in user inquiries that can inform product improvements.

 - Offer constructive feedback to enhance user and agent experiences within the application workflow.

 - Flex outside of core D-SNP advisory as needed, supporting broader Medicare enrollment efforts, general app CX, and other team initiatives as we scale into new states and markets.


THIS ROLE MIGHT NOT BE THE RIGHT FIT IF

We believe in being upfront so you can make the best decision for yourself. Propel is a startup, and our healthcare team is still building many of the processes and playbooks that larger organizations already have in place. That is exciting, but it is not for everyone, and that is completely okay.

This role may not be the right fit if:

 - You prefer a structured environment with established workflows and de

source_jobs.title: Senior Solutions Architect, Government Partnerships
source_jobs.description:
About Us

Propel is on a mission to empower low-income Americans by simplifying access to government benefits with modern technology. We're a passionate team of 100 Propellers - all working to help our users get through the month, every month.



About the Role

We're looking for a Solutions Architect to bridge Propel's Government Partnerships team and our Product & Engineering organization - translating state partners' needs into product strategy, and then owning the delivery of that work with states. This role has the opportunity to shape the way our product evolves with deeper government partnerships and increase the value we’re able to deliver to both users and government partners.

This is a hands-on IC role. You'll be the person writing the scoping doc, building the dashboard, sitting in the state meeting, configuring the campaign, and running the evaluation. You own both strategy and execution end to end. Over time, as this work scales, the role has potential to grow into a leadership position — but today we need someone who is excited about the execution.

You'll work across the state partnership funnel, from early relationship-building through campaign delivery. You should be the kind of person who can walk into a meeting with a state SNAP agency, understand their operational pain points around payment error rates or portal access, walk out with a concrete plan that maps to Propel's product roadmap — and then go execute it. The role reports into our Government Partnerships team, with a dotted line to Product.



Here's how you'll impact the company:

 - Be the product voice in state conversations. Prepare materials, present Propel's capabilities, and field technical questions from state partners directly. Translate what you learn into structured requirements and recommendations for product and engineering.

 - Own state campaign delivery end to end.
   
   - Define what Propel will deliver for each state engagement, write the scope-of-work, set up campaign infrastructure, monitor execution, run the evaluation, and fix what breaks.
   
   - Lean into AI-enablement to create more leverage for our partnerships work with less engineering support.
   
   - Make hands-on tradeoff decisions about when a state's needs warrant new product investment vs. existing capabilities — and when it's configuration work, do it yourself.

 - Feed the case management product roadmap. Synthesize patterns across engagements to surface common needs and platform gaps. Bring specific, evidence-based recommendations to PMs — not just "states want X," but "here's the pattern across three states, here's what a platform-level solution looks like, and here's the data."



You Might Be a Great Fit If You Have

 - 5+ years bridging product/technical teams and external partners — solutions architecture, implementation/delivery leadership, technical program management, or product management in a B2G context. You should have direct experience working in or selling to state SNAP or Medicaid agencies.

 - Technical background – you’re comfortable getting in the weeds on how our tech stack operates to tackle questions around how we should integrate with state partners. You are comfortable reading API docs, translating technical documents for non-technical stakeholders and doing a first-draft estimate of complexity and scope for new tasks.

 - Familiarity with public benefits administration — SNAP, Medicaid, EBT, or adjacent programs. You understand (or can quickly learn) how state agencies work, what their incentives are, and why things like payment error rates and redetermination processes matter

 - The ability to operate across altitudes — presenting to a state CIO on Monday, speccing a campaign config on Tuesday, analyzing pilot results on Wednesday, drafting a SOW on Thursday

 - Strong product instincts — you can tell the difference between a state request that should become a platform feature and one that should stay a one-off. You know when to advoc

source_jobs.title: GTM Strategy & Operations Lead
source_jobs.description:
About Us

Propel is on a mission to empower low-income Americans by simplifying access to government benefits with modern technology. We're a passionate team of 100 Propellers - all working to help our users get through the month, every month.

The Role

We launched the Propel Marketplace to connect our ~5M monthly active users to products, services, and exclusive offers from leading brands across grocery and retail, healthcare, telecoms, financial services, and auto. It has grown into a powerful, industry-leading performance tool for our partners and a critical revenue driver for Propel. We have strong product market fit, our users love our app, and our customers love that we have a significant reach and engagement with our users.

We’re looking for a GTM Strategy and Operations Lead! We're scaling revenue operations for a profitable marketplace - and we need someone who moves fast, thinks strategically, and is eager to provide proactive support to senior leaders.

You'll own the RevOps program end-to-end: embedding AI into our tech stack, surfacing insights that drive decisions, and serving as a trusted partner to the Customer Success and Business Development teams. You stay ahead of industry trends and hold yourself to a high bar.

This is a high-visibility role on our Marketplace Strategy & Operations team, reporting to the Director of Strategy & Operations. You’ll work closely with the GM of Marketplace, Head of CS, and Head of BD & Sales.

Here's how you'll impact the company

 - Power GTM planning: Lead comp planning and goal-setting in partnership with Sales, CS, and the GM as we push toward our next growth milestone

 - Drive analytical rigor: Diagnose performance gaps with hypothesis-driven analysis, build dashboards that surface what actually matters, and make actionable recommendations based on your findings

 - Own the GTM tech stack: Serve as our primary Salesforce admin and own the broader suite of tools that Sales and CS rely on - maintaining data integrity, driving adoption, and proactively identifying new tools (including AI-powered ones) that make the team more effective

 - Enable the team: Build processes that stick, standardize how we work across BD and CS, and establish AI usage guidelines that ensure consistency across team outputs so the team can move fast without creating fragmentation

Requirements

 - Experience: 3+ years in analytical roles supporting enterprise GTM teams (BizOps, Revenue Operations, Revenue Enablement, etc.). Experience at growth stage company (50-200 employees) strongly preferred

 - Analytical rigor: You take a hypothesis-driven approach, know how to frame a question well, and deliver findings with clear logic and a point of view

 - Self-directed: Comfortable prioritizing in an ambiguous environment and picking up new tools and contexts quickly

 - AI fluency: You proactively explore ways to use AI tools to improve team workflows and GTM processes. You know when and where to use them to accelerate great judgment, not just to move faster

 - Documentation: Your materials are self-explanatory, well-structured, and formatted for the audience

 - GTM tech stack: Must be comfortable as a primary admin of Salesforce - building, maintaining, and improving the instance. Knowledge and curiosity for other GTM tools

 - Experience in ads, ad tech, or performance marketing is a plus

At Propel, you'll enjoy

 - Meaningful work and a strong shared sense of mission

 - Remote-friendly work environment with a dog-friendly office in Downtown Brooklyn. The team meets in-person for optional retreats.

 - 20 Days of PTO

 - 4% 401K match

 - $10k lifetime spend towards Carrot Fertility

 - Excellent Medical, Dental and Vision options

 - Other health and mental health focused benefits and perks

 - Propel believes that everyone should be compensated fairly and equitably. We set our salary ranges using compensation data from hundreds of NYC-based startups at our stage. Additionally, pay is not det

source_jobs.title: Product Lead, Grocery
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT PROPEL

Propel is on a mission to build technology that strengthens the social safety net. We’re a passionate team of ~100 Propellers who envision a future where every American has the tools and resources they need to thrive.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We launched the Propel Marketplace to connect our ~5M monthly active users to products, services, and exclusive offers from leading brands across grocery and retail, healthcare, telecoms, financial services, and auto. It has grown into a powerful, industry-leading performance tool for our partners and a critical revenue driver for Propel. We have strong product market fit, our users love our app, and our customers love that we have a significant reach and engagement with our users.

We're hiring a Product Lead (Senior or Staff Product Manager) to own the Grocery product vertical that sits within our broader Marketplace product ecosystem. You'll inherit and drive a large cross-functional team (engineers, designers, and data scientists) with a clear problem space and momentum. In addition to running a cross-functional product squad, you'll partner closely with our business team across business development and customer success. You’ll report directly to the VP of Product.

We’re looking for someone who is an excellent executor and is excited to drive user and commercial strategy. You'll ship — fast, with quality, across a tight cross functional pod — and you'll also shape the bets: which user segments we build for, how we translate retailer and CPG demand into a product users love, and where the durable value (and revenue) actually comes from. We’re looking for someone who is excited and energized by the opportunity to balance commercial strategy with low income user experiences.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own the end-to-end product vision & strategy for grocery – both how we help users save money on groceries and how we create value for commercial partners

 - Deeply understand the grocery shopping habits & needs of SNAP recipients & be able to identify areas of opportunity & overlap with retailers & CPGs

 - Balance a portfolio of smaller experiments and larger 0 →1 efforts that define our roadmap in the space – this looks like sharp hypothesis definition, fast iteration and close collaboration with engineering, design & sales

 - Define & track the success metrics that tie user value to commercial performance

 - Communicate product strategy clearly to leadership, cross-functional teams and commercial partners

 - Uses AI tools (we use Claude) fluently to move faster - turning a rough brief into a structured doc, a messy transcript into clear action items, or a vague ask into a scoped problem statement




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - 5+ years of product management experience with direct experience shipping large-scale consumer products

 - Commercial fluency & partnership experience. You’ve had to work with commercial partners before (ideally in the retail space), and gone through the exercise of identifying areas of overlap in user and partner needs. You can sit across the table from a retailer or CPG, understand what they need, and translate requirements into the product — without losing sight of user value.

 - Demonstrated 0→1 product experience. You’ve taken a product from an idea or early concept to a shipped, scaled solution.

 - AI fluency. You use AI tools fluently as part of daily work - prompting effectively, evaluating outputs critically, and applying them where they genuinely accelerate the work, whether that's synthesizing research, structuring docs, or turning ambiguous asks into scoped problem statements.

 - Data-driven. You reach for the data yourself (we run on Amplitude, Metabase/Snowflake), frame the right metrics, and can tell the difference between a real signal and a vanity one.

 - Mission-aligned. You care about building for low-income and underserved users, and you bring both empathy and rigor to it. You hold a high bar that behavior change has to be worth i

notes: Mission-driven company with a strong engineering core, focused on building technology for social safety net.
